explanation:
Family well-being and healthy child development involve multiple dimensions: health, education, and digital wellness. Each developmental stage brings unique considerations.

## How It's Calculated

Daily screen time limits based on age · Free and instant.

This calculator applies evidence-based guidelines using your input values, checking against recommended ranges and alerting you if values fall outside typical recommendations.

## Final Notes

This calculation is for guidance only. For critical decisions about your child's health and development, consult a pediatrician or specialist. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: How much screen time for babies under 2?
faq.a: Avoid screen time except for video calls with family members.

faq.q: What's the screen time limit for ages 2-5?
faq.a: Maximum 1 hour daily of high-quality programming, like educational content (Sesame Street).

faq.q: How much screen time for ages 6-12?
faq.a: Up to 2 hours daily with consistent limits and quality content focus.

faq.q: What about screen time for teenagers?
faq.a: Screen time should be agreed upon and focused on quality content and healthy digital habits.

faq.q: Can kids have more screen time on weekends?
faq.a: Occasional exceptions are okay, but maintain consistency and moderation throughout the week.
Yes, all our calculators are completely free with no account or registration required.
These guidelines are based on AAP (American Academy of Pediatrics) standards. For individual concerns about your child, consult a pediatrician.

faq.q: Do you store my personal data?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We don't send any data to our servers.
